name: Onion Blossoms
recipeInstructions:
1. Leaving the root end intact, peel the outer skin of the onion.
2. Cut a small slice off the top.
3. Starting at the top of the onion and on one side, make a cut downward toward the root end, stopping 1/2 inch from the bottom.
4. Make additional cuts 1/8 inch from the first until there are cuts completely across the top of the onion.
5. Turn the onion a quarter turn so the slices are horizontal to you.
6. Repeat the cuts 1/8 inch apart from each other until there is a checkerboard pattern across the entire top of the onion.
7. In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, sour cream, chili powder, and 1 1/2 teaspoons Cajun seasoning. Mix well and set aside.
8. In a 1-gallon plastic bag, combine flour and remaining Cajun seasoning.
9. Place milk in a small bowl.
10. Coat the cut onion in flour, then dip into milk and back into the flour mixture.
11. Fry in oil at 350°F for 5 minutes or until golden, turning once.
12. Remove from oil; place on serving plate.
13. Discard the very center of the fried onion blossom.
14. Place a few spoonfuls of dip into the center of the blossom and serve immediately.
